description_long: This object is a large, industrial radio receiver tower, commonly seen in urban environments as part of telecommunications infrastructure. It features a central cylindrical mast supporting two distinct circular arrays of rectangular antenna panels, each array positioned at different heights along the mast. The antenna panels are evenly spaced around the circumference, connected by a lattice of metal support struts and cross-bracing for structural stability. Additional smaller dish antennas and electronic equipment are mounted on the lower array, indicating its function as a multi-purpose transmission and receiving station. The surface appears weathered, with a predominantly metallic finish and some areas of discoloration or corrosion. The overall structure is robust, designed to withstand outdoor conditions and support heavy electronic components.
description: A tall, cylindrical radio receiver tower with two circular arrays of rectangular antenna panels and metal lattice supports, featuring additional dish antennas and electronic equipment.
description_view_0: This view highlights the upper and lower circular arrays of antenna panels, showing the dense arrangement of rectangular panels and the central mast.
description_view_1: From this angle, the symmetry of the antenna arrays is clear, and the lattice structure connecting the panels is prominent.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the vertical alignment of the mast and the staggered positioning of the antenna panels, with some dish antennas visible.
description_view_3: This view provides a clear look at the lower array's electronic equipment and the overall cylindrical form of the mast, with the antenna panels encircling it.
